channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: STAT+: A ‘historic’ FDA clearance raises the question: Is LLM the interface? Or the decision-maker?
item.description: The newly cleared device is based around an app that helps a patient manage their diabetes using a treatment plan defined by their doctor.

item.title: Takeda and insilico strike ai-led drug discovery deal worth up to $600m
item.description: HONG KONG -- Japanese pharmaceutical giant Takeda has reached a deal with Hong Kong-based Insilico Medicine to use the biotechnology company's artificial intelligence platform to identify potential new drugs. Collaboration underlines surge in cross-border bio…

item.title: How proteins fold
item.description: Despite advances in protein structure prediction, how proteins fold remains unclear. Understanding protein (mis)folding will require single-molecule techniques and advanced computation tools.

item.title: People behind the progress: preparing us for what comes next
item.description: The TechUK President’s Awards celebrate individuals across the tech sector who are helping to transform the world we live in for the better

item.title: Pre-deployment simulations are vital to developing generative AI that can best provide mental health advice
item.description: Pre-deployment simulation is a new technique from OpenAI. It can be used to better shape AI-led mental health guidance. An AI Insider analysis and scoop.

item.title: 5 under-the-radar stocks fueling the AI revolution
item.description: Nvidia was the first poster child of the AI revolution. OpenAI turned ChatGPT into a global phenomenon. Google, Microsoft, and Anthropic have since dominated headlines with ever-more-powerful models. But throughout history, the biggest fortunes made during a …

item.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30m of his own money to build AI alternative to microsoft office | techcrunch
item.description: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office, Google Apps with AI.

item.title: UN warns of need for global governance to avoid an ai-pocalypse
item.description: Capabilities are racing ahead of rules to ensure tech is used safely and responsibly, Scientific Panel argues

item.title: Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy reprograms CNS immunity and alters Alzheimer's biomarkers: results from two open-label clinical trials
item.description: Two open-label clinical trials demonstrate that Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) immunotherapy modulates central nervous system immunity and produces measurable changes in Alzheimer's disease biomarkers. These findings suggest BCG may alter the immune environment in the brain through a reprogramming m

item.title: Dissecting autonomous enzymatic variability in single cells
item.description: Researchers used single-cell analysis to characterize the inherent variability in enzyme activity across individual cells, providing insights into how enzymatic function differs at the cellular level independent of external factors.

item.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
item.description: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.

item.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
item.description: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.

item.title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give m

item.title: Targeted enzyme discovery using metal-coordination mining
item.description: A methodology for mining protein structure databases on the basis of the distinct intrinsic structures of metal-binding active sites in enzymes enables the discovery of new families of radical halogenases.

item.title: Towards the construction of a virtual yeast
item.description: A virtual yeast, an artificial intelligence-driven agent for modelling eukaryotic cellular behaviours, is described.

item.title: Kpmg ran an internal exam to certify staff on the ethical use of artificial intelligence — 28 employees were caught using artificial intelligence to cheat on it, including a partner who was fined $10,000
item.description: KPMG Australia confirmed in February 2026 that 28 of its staff had used artificial intelligence tools to cheat on internal training exams since July 2025, after the firm introduced monitoring systems specifically designed to detect unauthorised AI use during …

item.title: U2af1 mutations rescue deleterious exon skipping induced by kras mutations
item.description: KRASG12S mutations generate potential docking sites for splicing factor U2AF1, which can lead to exon skipping and nonfunctional KRAS transcripts. This is mitigated by mutations in U2AF1, which rescue exon skipping, thereby restoring KRAS activity in lung ade…

item.title: What cios need to know about AI notetaking security
item.description: AI meeting tools enhance collaboration and efficiency but introduce serious privacy, compliance and data governance risks.

item.title: AI agents are making marketing decisions on data no one has checked in years
item.description: Ask anyone in ad tech about data governance, and they’ll talk about the supply side. The industry spent years building verification frameworks for publishers and sellers, proving that the data powering supply-side decisions is what it claims to be. Standards …

item.title: Review highlights strong safety and effectiveness of mrna vaccines
item.description: A comprehensive new review published in The Lancet affirms that currently approved mRNA vaccines are safe and highly effective at preventing infectious diseases, including COVID-19.

item.title: Biological aging might help to explain the rising risk of early-onset cancer
item.description: Cancers have risen rapidly in younger adults in recent generations. Analyzing large population-based cohorts, we found that recent generations exhibited signs of faster biological aging than older ones, and that systemic and organ-specific aging markers were …

item.title: Trop2 targeting reveals therapy-driven cell state dynamics in colorectal cancer
item.description: TROP2 marks poor-prognosis colorectal cancer cells with fetal-like, therapy-resistant states, and therapeutic targeting of TROP2 with antibody–drug conjugates in combination with chemotherapy enhances treatment efficacy by modulating tumour cell plasticity an…

item.title: Improved heritability partitioning and enrichment analyses using summary statistics with graphreml
item.description: graphREML is a likelihood-based heritability partitioning method that uses genome-wide association studies summary statistics and linkage disequilibrium graphical models. It yields enrichment estimates concordant with S-LDSC but shows greater power and produc…

item.title: Electronic health and care records specialist group bi-monthly webinar
item.description: Join our bi-monthly EHCR Community of Practice meetings to share innovation, advance shared care records, and tackle health inequalities. Join us on Thursday, 21 January 2027 from 12:00pm.

item.title: Trump drops restrictions on anthropic's mythos and fable models | techcrunch
item.description: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined fable 5 is greenlit to return
item.description: After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access tomorrow. Anthropic: We've received notice that the Departme…

item.title: Gabapentin Extended Release Tablets in Healthy Subjects Under Fed Conditions: A Randomized, Open-Label, Two-Treatment, Two-Period, Two-Sequence, Single Dose, Crossover, Comparative Bioavailability Study
item.description: This randomized, open-label, crossover bioavailability study compared gabapentin extended-release tablets in healthy subjects under fed conditions using a two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence design with single-dose administration. The study was conducted by Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Limited

item.title: Cytotoxicity and synergistic anticancer activity of Kigelia africana (Lam.) Benth. and Spathodea campanulata P. Beauv. polyherbal extracts
item.description: This study evaluated the cytotoxic and anticancer properties of polyherbal extracts combining Kigelia africana and Spathodea campanulata, investigating their individual and synergistic effects against cancer cell lines. The research demonstrates that these plant extracts exhibit cytotoxic activity a

item.title: FDA Approves First Gene Therapy for Young Children with Sickle Cell Disease
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a supplemental approval for Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) for patients aged 2 years and older with either sickle cell disease (SCD) with recurrent vaso-occlusive crises (VOCs) or transfusion-dependent β thalassemia (TDT).

item.title: Statins and blood pressure drugs changing health risks of obesity, study suggests
item.description: New research shows obesity patients over 40 are managing blood pressure and cholesterol as well as healthy-weight peers, but younger adults remain at risk.

item.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about Anthropic drug development goals, Trump drug plan snags, and more
item.description: Anthropic announced the availability of Claude Science, which optimizes its large language model for use in scientific labs and by drugmakers

item.title: STAT+: More signs that GLP-1s may help with peripheral artery disease
item.description: Anthropic will begin developing drugs, BridgeBio pockets a billion, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: FDA says Zyn can market its pouches as safer than cigarettes
item.description: The influence of the alcohol industry on policy, AI Claude for scientists, and more health news from Morning Rounds

item.title: STAT+: FDA approves Orca Bio’s T cell therapy for blood cancer patients
item.description: The FDA approved a T-cell therapy from Orca Bio that reduces the risk of a debilitating immune reaction in patients with blood cancers undergoing stem cell transplants.

item.title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
item.description: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…

item.title: The Download: Anthropic launches Claude Science, and California’s carbon manure math
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers yesterday, Anthropi

item.title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
item.description: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.

item.title: 12 immigrant founders driving america’s a.i. boom
item.description: From Elon Musk to Fei-Fei Li, founders who came to the U.S. are driving the companies and research behind today’s multibillion-dollar A.I. boom.

item.title: UN warns that AI safety is lagging behind AI progress
item.description: While artificial intelligence (AI)’s capabilities are expanding, a new United Nations (U.N.) report says its safety rules are lacking. The preliminary report by the U.N. Independent International Scientific Panel on Artificial Intelligence published Wednesday…

item.title: As AI models become commodities, one bio-native AI company just moved to patent the thing that actually compounds: the data
item.description: Issued on behalf of MindWalk Holdings Corp. MindWalk Holdings Corp. (NASDAQ: HYFT) filed a foundational European patent application covering the multi-dimensional data architecture at the core of its HYFT® Technology, an attempt to protect, at the architectur…

item.title: AI company anthropic unveils new drug discovery program
item.description: Anthropic announced that it is launching new drug discovery programs in an attempt to produce artificial intelligence-powered tools created for drugmakers.

item.title: Towards globally equitable bioinformatics adoption
item.description: Advances in AI-driven bioinformatics promise democratized discovery, yet major inequities persist. This Perspective uses AlphaFold as an illustrative case to argue that equitable adoption of bioinformatics technologies will require sustained global investment…

item.title: NIH unveils the world’s largest genomics-and-health database
item.description: The US government has just handed scientists the largest map of human health ever assembled. It pairs more than half a million genomes with real medical records, and it arrives as the programme behind it faces deep budget cuts. The database comes from All of …

item.title: AI explained: why the world needs to act now
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is moving faster than governments can keep up. Read Full Story on UN News The post AI explained: Why the world needs to act now appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

item.title: Courts split on whether AI training is fair use
item.description: The first wave of artificial intelligence (AI) regulation focused on what goes into models. California’s Training Data Transparency Act, which went into effect Jan. 1, requires developers to disclose their training data, including sources, collection periods…

item.title: How inscribe uses amazon bedrock to stop document fraud in seconds
item.description: In this post, you will learn how Inscribe developed an agentic AI system using Amazon Bedrock that reasons across documents the way an expert fraud analyst would. With this new agentic AI system, Inscribe now detects tampered, fabricated, and AI-generated fin…

item.title: One rare bipartisan AI bill is moving through congress. here’s why it deserves to pass
item.description: The CREATE AI Act doesn't pick winners, doesn't burden industry and already has a proven track record — Capitol Hill's most actionable AI legislation.

item.title: How anthropic, openai, the vatican and congress want to govern AI
item.description: Anthropic, OpenAI, the Vatican and Congress all agree AI needs guardrails—but they disagree on what should be protected first, from catastrophic risk to human dignity and U.S. competitiveness.

item.title: Who decides when a cyber AI tool is safe to deploy?
item.description: As AI cyber threats grow, organizations need trained teams, not just stronger tools.

item.title: Insightfultake | aarogya setu 2.0: india's covid tracing app reinvents itself as a digital health platform
item.description: Aarogya Setu 2.0 transforms India's Covid tracing app into a digital health platform with ABHA integration, AI-powered records, and new privacy concerns.
